operating_state: Net revenue reached $4.3 billion for Q3 2021, representing 54% growth driven by Computing and Graphics segment growth of 44% and Enterprise segment growth of 69%. Computing and Graphics growth reflected 83% higher average selling prices from richer Ryzen, Radeon and AMD Instinct product mix, partially offset by lower unit shipments due to strategic focus on premium products in tight supply environment. Enterprise growth was driven by higher EPYC server processor and semi-custom product sales including PlayStation and Xbox platforms. International sales represented 67% of net revenue with manufacturing relying on third-party foundries primarily TSMC for 7nm production.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved to 48% from 44% in prior year period, primarily driven by richer mix of EPYC, Ryzen and Radeon processor sales. Research and development and marketing expenses increased substantially due to expanded headcount, higher employee incentives, increased go-to-market activities and Xilinx acquisition-related costs. Operating income reached $948 million compared to $449 million in prior year, as strong revenue growth more than offset higher operating expenses.
material_events: Company repurchased 7.2 million shares for $750 million during the quarter under the $4 billion share repurchase program, with $3 billion remaining available. AMD amended its GLOBALFOUNDRIES wafer supply agreement removing exclusivity commitments while securing minimum capacity allocations through 2024 with annual purchase targets and prepayment requirements. Holders converted $25 million principal amount of convertible notes into approximately 3 million shares, reducing debt balances.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues impacting global supply chain causing disruptions to service providers and logistics flow. Operations face political, legal and economic risks across international markets including trade protection measures, export licensing requirements, and currency exchange rate changes. Chinese entity restrictions continue affecting the THATIC joint venture following BIS Entity List designation. Cryptocurrency mining demand volatility continued affecting GPU revenue with regulatory restrictions in South Korea and China creating market uncertainty.
forward_outlook: Management expects international sales to continue as significant portion of total sales with company making strategic investments in long-term supply chain capacity to support future revenue growth amid strong demand. Xilinx acquisition currently expected to close by end of calendar year 2021, subject to remaining regulatory approvals. Management expressed confidence in liquidity position with $3.6 billion in cash and short-term investments, supported by strong operating cash flow generation.
uncertainty_profile: Xilinx merger completion remains subject to regulatory approvals from certain foreign jurisdictions with potential termination fees depending on circumstances, and regulatory approval deadline extended to April 2022. Multiple ongoing patent litigation matters create potential liability exposure. Key uncertainties include potential penalties if annual wafer purchase targets with GLOBALFOUNDRIES are not met and supply chain disruptions from semiconductor industry consolidation.
